title: syncodon glomeratum
intro_study: According to "International journal of impotence research", Syncodon glomeratum is not mentioned in the study, but Syncodon glomeratum is a plant which is believed to be a synonym for Syncolpremis glomerata which is also known as  Korean red ginseng, and according to the study, Korean red ginseng benefits include significant improvements in early detumescence, penile rigidity and girth, libido, and patient satisfactions for treating erectile dysfunction.
side_effects: Syncodon glomeratum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, which may include skin irritation, itching, and rashes in some individuals. Overconsumption of the plant's extracts can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Furthermore, using Syncodon glomeratum in high concentrations or for extended periods can result in liver damage and potentially even kidney failure in severe cases. Additionally, people with pre-existing medical conditions, such as liver or kidney disease, may experience exacerbated symptoms if they use Syncodon glomeratum improperly.
